cancer_history_summary:
Age: 19  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Osteosarcoma (high‑grade conventional) of the distal right femur  
Histology: High‑grade conventional osteosarcoma  

Current extent: Metastatic – solitary solid pulmonary nodule in the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe (measures 2.0 × 1.8 cm) representing radiographic progression of metastatic disease  

Biomarkers: No genomic or protein expression results reported; comprehensive sarcoma‑focused next‑generation sequencing has been ordered on a forthcoming biopsy specimen  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 2024: Definitive surgical resection of distal right femur (wide excision with prosthetic reconstruction) – curative intent, stage IIB, ypT2b N0 M0  
# 2024‑mid‑2025 (completed by May 2025): Adjuvant MAP chemotherapy (high‑dose Methotrexate, Doxorubicin, Cisplatin) – six cycles, completed without major complication  
# Pending (post‑June 2025): Image‑guided biopsy of right lower‑lobe pulmonary nodule; multidisciplinary planning for either video‑assisted thoracoscopic wedge resection or stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT); subsequent systemic therapy to be determined following pathology and molecular testing
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, hepatic dysfunction, HIV infection, or chronic viral hepatitis.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma (left upper extremity primary)  
Histology: Malignant melanoma, BRAFV600E-mutated, NRAS wild‑type  
Current extent: Metastatic disease confined to bilateral pulmonary nodules (measurable disease); partial response to therapy as of latest imaging (largest nodule 11 mm, ↓33%).  

Biomarkers: BRAFV600E mutation positive; NRAS wild‑type; LDH modestly elevated at 285 U/L (baseline) decreasing to 185 U/L on repeat labs; thyroid function normal; no PD‑L1 reported.  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 2022 – Surgical resection: wide local excision of left upper extremity melanoma with sentinel lymph node dissection showing 2/3 nodes positive with extracapsular extension.  
# 2023‑04 to 2024‑04 (approximately 12 months): Adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks. Completed 12 months without radiographic recurrence; experienced Grade 2 dermatologic toxicity (rash) at month 6, resolved with topical steroids.  
# 2025‑06‑22 onward: Frontline combined checkpoint inhibition – ipilimumab 3 mg/kg IV + nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks (planned 4 induction cycles).  
 • Cycle 1 (started 2025‑06‑22): tolerated, Grade 1 pruritus.  
 • Cycle 2 (administered ≈2025‑07‑xx): uneventful. Post‑Cycle 2 imaging (2025‑06‑23) showed partial response (≈33% tumor shrinkage).  
 • Cycle 3 (≈2025‑08‑xx): precipitated Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis requiring hospitalisation; ipilimumab permanently discontinued.  
# 2025‑08‑xx to present: Nivolumab monotherapy 240 mg IV q2 weeks (maintenance). Continuing as of 2025‑06‑25 with ongoing partial response; mild fatigue (Grade 1) and intermittent dry mouth noted; no further severe irAEs.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases.  
No documentation of congestive heart failure.  
No pneumonitis reported on imaging or exam.  
Renal function normal (Creatinine 0.92 mg/dL).  
Liver enzymes within normal limits (AST 31 U/L, ALT 34 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL).  
No recorded HIV or chronic hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55  
Sex: Female  

Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: Low‑grade serous carcinoma (FIGO Stage IIIC at initial presentation)  

Current extent: Recurrent/progressive disease confined to peritoneum and pelvic/retro‑aortic lymph nodes (FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall implants and a 2 cm para‑aortic node); no distant organ or bony metastases identified on imaging to date.  

Biomarkers: KRAS p.G12D dominant clone (variant allele frequency ≈45 %), co‑existent NRAS Q61K subclone (low VAF), CCNE1 copy‑number gain (~3‑fold). Serum CA‑125 elevated and rising (historical values: 85→118 U/mL →210 U/mL in late May 2025; subsequent value 62 U/mL ↑ from 48 U/mL in early June 2025).  

Treatment history:  
# Surgery – Total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑oophorectomy with comprehensive staging performed at age 53 (≈2023).  
# Chemotherapy – Carboplatin (AUC 5) + Paclitaxel (175 mg/m²) × 4 cycles, completed circa Feb 2024 (14 months prior to 2025‑06‑22).  
# Endocrine therapy – Letrozole 2.5 mg orally once daily, initiated March 2025; ongoing as of latest note (2025‑06‑25). Best response: Initial rise in CA‑125 and emergence of new FDG‑avid pelvic lesions suggested progression shortly after start; however repeat CT in June 2025 demonstrated stable peritoneal implants and unchanged retro‑aortic node, indicating at least disease stabilization while on letrozole.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (Cr 0.85 mg/dL), hepatic dysfunction (AST/ALT within normal limits), or HIV/hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 45  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma (mediastinal primary)  
Histology: Precursor T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma, TdT⁺, CD3⁺, CD7⁺  
Current extent: Metastatic/Systemic disease with residual mediastinal mass, bilateral pulmonary nodules, multiple hepatic lesions, probable splenic and bone‑marrow involvement (progressive per Lugano classification)  

Biomarkers: CD20−, CD19−, CD22−, NOTCH1 V1578F mutation, PTEN loss, JAK3 V678M mutation, TAL1‑ST5 fusion; LDH 620 U/L (↑); AST/ALT >5 × ULN (grade 3 transaminitis)  

Treatment history:  
# Front‑line Hyper‑CVAD ± high‑dose MTX/cytarabine – 4 cycles (completed prior to 2025, resulted in partial response; associated grade 3 oral mucositis)  
# Salvage nelarabine – 2 cycles (administered after Hyper‑CVAD, achieved stable disease then progression)  
# Palliative mediastinal external beam radiation – 30 Gy in 10 fractions (course completed by early June 2025; interim CT 2025‑06‑23 showed ≈70 % tumor volume reduction)  
# Prednisone 40 mg PO daily × 5 days with taper (started 2025‑06‑22 for hepatic inflammation)  
# Anticoagulation for right popliteal/femoral DVT – therapeutic enoxaparin 1 mg/kg SC q12h initiated ≈mid‑May 2025, transitioned to apixaban 5 mg PO BID (ongoing as of 2025‑06‑22)  
# Neuropathic pain management – gabapentin 300 mg PO t.i.d.; bortezomib 1.0 mg/m² SC on Days 1, 8, 15 of each 28‑day cycle planned (dose reduced because of grade 2 peripheral neuropathy); vincristine slated for discontinuation after Cycle 1 if neuropathy worsened (both discussed 2025‑06‑22, actual administration not recorded)  
# Investigational considerations (not yet commenced): gamma‑secretase inhibitor (NOTCH1‑mutant), ruxolitinib (JAK3 V678M), PI3Kδ inhibitor, CD3‑bispecific antibody, CAR‑T targeting TRBV, off‑label pembrolizumab (discussed 2025‑06‑25)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
Evidence of liver dysfunction – transaminitis with AST/ALT >5 × upper limit of normal documented on 2025‑06‑22 (and persisting on subsequent assessments).  
No documentation of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, or HIV/hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: High‑grade serous carcinoma (FIGO Stage IV)  
Current extent: Metastatic disease, presently radiographically stable (no new abdominal masses, stable hepatic metastases, fibrotic para‑aortic node, no ascites)  
Biomarkers: CA‑125 48 U/mL (stable vs prior 44 U/mL)  

Treatment history:  
# 03/2025: Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (lesion reduced from 4.2 cm to 3.8 cm with post‑treatment necrosis) – outcome: stable hepatic disease on follow‑up CT 2 weeks later  
# Early 2025 (approximate): Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy in 5 fractions – outcome: fibrosis, no measurable enhancement on subsequent imaging  
# Ongoing since circa 2024 (exact start not specified): Rucaparib 600 mg orally twice daily – tolerated; grade 2 anemia/fatigue managed, continues as of 20 Jun 2025 with no new toxicities
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, clinically significant renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.84 mg/dL), clinically significant liver dysfunction (AST 38 U/L, ALT 42 U/L, ALP 112 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL), HIV infection, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 63  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung carcinoma (extensive‑stage)  
Histology: Small‑cell carcinoma  
Current extent: Progressive disease – new solitary hepatic metastasis (segment VII, 1.6 cm, SUVmax 7.8) with previously stable primary lung mass, mediastinal nodes, left adrenal nodule, and right femoral lytic lesion; no CNS disease.  

Biomarkers: Not reported  

Treatment history:  
# First‑line (dates not specifically recorded): carboplatin AUC 5 + etoposide 100 mg/m² IV days 1–3 every 21 days, total 4 cycles; concurrent palliative external beam radiation to L3 (single 8 Gy fraction). Achieved partial response by Cycle 2 CT (≈30 % tumor shrinkage). Experienced Grade 3 neutropenia (treated with filgrastim), Grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring transfusion, and Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy.  

# Second‑line (initiated after completion of first‑line, exact dates not recorded): lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² IV every 21 days. Received ≥5 cycles. After 3 cycles showed modest decrease in adrenal size and stable femur lesion; developed Grade 2 anemia (Hb 9.0 g/dL) managed with oral iron. Subsequent Grade 3 neutropenia led to dose reduction after fifth cycle. Disease remained stable until restaging PET‑CT demonstrated new hepatic metastasis meeting RECIST 1.1 criteria for progressive disease.  

# Planned fourth‑line (documented but not yet started as of 2025‑06‑25): temozolomide monotherapy 200 mg/m² orally days 1‑5 every 28 days.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, clinically significant renal dysfunction, severe liver dysfunction beyond mild impairment related to hepatic metastasis, HIV infection, or active hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 62  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Glioblastoma, right parietal lobe (brain)  
Histology: IDH‑wildtype glioblastoma

Biomarkers: IDH‑wildtype (reported)  

Treatment history:

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Breast cancer  
Primary site: Right breast  
Histology: Invasive lobular carcinoma (hormone‑receptor‑positive, HER2‑low)  

Current extent: Metastatic (known hepatic lesion in segment IV and multiple osseous metastases in thoracic spine); disease classified as stable/partial response to current therapy  

Biomarkers:   
- Estrogen/receptor positivity (implied by HR⁺ designation)  
- HER2‑low (no HER2 amplification)  
- PIK3CA activating mutation E545K  
- ESR1 mutation D538G  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 2023 (patient age 64) – Initiated oral capecitabine 1250 mg/m² twice daily Days 1‑14 every 21 days; ongoing as of 6/20/2025. Best radiographic response: partial response per RECIST v1.1, disease stable on CT (hepatic lesion reduced from 1.8 cm to ~1.5 cm) and bone scans.  
# Prior to capecitabine – Received paclitaxel (taxane‑based chemotherapy) ending >2 years before 6/2025; resulted in residual Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy.  
(Endocrine therapies were implied given HR⁺ status but not specifically dated.)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No documented uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, clinically significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or active hepatitis reported in the available excerpts.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 34  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Appendiceal neuroendocrine tumour (well‑differentiated gastroenteropancreatic NET)  
Histology: Well‑differentiated, Grade 2 (Ki‑67 ≈ 8%)  
Current extent: Metastatic to liver (two small hepatic lesions, stable disease)  
Biomarkers: Somatostatin receptor‑2 positive (⁶⁸Ga‑DOTATATE SUVmax ≈ 12); Ki‑67 ~8%  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 2015 (age 24): Surgical resection of primary appendix NET (Stage IA, T1 N0 M0) – definitive local therapy.  
# Approx. 2016‑2018 (ages 25‑27): Temozolomide + Bevacizumab – partial radiographic response of peritoneal nodules. Discontinued once disease stabilised.  
# Approx. 2018‑present: Octreotide LAR 30 mg intramuscular every 28 days – initiated after confirmation of SSTR2 positivity; continuously maintained through all subsequent visits.  
# Approx. 2022 (age 31): Lu‑177‑DOTATATE peptide receptor radionuclide therapy (PRRT) – completed; resulted in regression of several hepatic lesions and durable disease control elsewhere.  
# 03/2025‑present: Everolimus 10 mg orally daily combined with ongoing Octreotide LAR – commenced following detection of a new 1 cm hepatic metastasis; latest MRI (June 2025) shows stable disease per RECIST.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection reported in the available records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Gastro‑esophageal junction adenocarcinoma  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma  
Current extent: Metastatic disease with stable hepatic metastases, progressive peritoneal carcinomatosis, new moderate‑volume ascites, slight enlargement of retroperitoneal lymph nodes; overall assessment – progressive disease  

Biomarkers: HER2 positive (IHC 3+, ISH amplification); co‑amplifications MET and FGFR2; KRAS p.G12D mutation; PIK3CA p.H1047R mutation; PD‑L1 CPS 15  

Treatment history:  
# First‑line (dates not explicitly recorded): CAPOX (capecitabine + oxaliplatin) plus trastuzumab – achieved disease stabilization.  
# Second‑line (dates not explicitly recorded): FOLFIRI (folinic acid + fluorouracil + irinotecan) – progressed.  
# Third‑line (ongoing as of 6/25/2025): Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg IV every 3 weeks – initiated prior to 2025, continued through 6/25/2025; latest dose administered 6 days before 6/25/2025; best response initially stable disease, now radiographic progression.  
# Radiation therapy: Localized external beam radiation to lumbar vertebral body L3 (osteoblastic metastatic focus) – completed prior to 6/25/2025; no new osseous lesions reported.  
# Supportive therapies: Levothyroxine 75 µg daily for immune‑related hypothyroidism (grade 2); intermittent ondansetron for nausea; ferrous sulfate 325 mg BID and planned epoetin alfa for anemia; serial red blood cell transfusions as required.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, clinically significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection documented in the supplied records.
